name: Wirken: binary write via write_file
query: logs("@ddsource:wirken @wirken.kind:assistant_tool_calls @wirken.event.calls.name:write_file (@write_file_extension:exe OR @write_file_extension:dll OR @write_file_extension:so OR @write_file_extension:dylib OR @write_file_extension:msi OR @write_file_extension:scr OR @write_file_extension:bat OR @write_file_extension:ps1 OR @write_file_extension:sh OR @write_file_extension:cmd OR @write_file_extension:com OR @write_file_extension:vbs OR @write_file_extension:jar OR @write_file_magic:*)").index("main").rollup("count").last("15m") > 0
message:
Wirken agent wrote a file matching a binary-extension allow-list or a content magic-byte prefix.

Detection: binary_write (3)
Severity baseline: high

Fields to pivot on: @wirken.session_id, @wirken.event.agent_id, @wirken.event.adapter_id, @wirken.event.sender_id, @write_file_path, @write_file_extension, @write_file_magic.

The `@write_file_extension` and `@write_file_magic` fields are materialised by a Datadog log parsing pipeline that JSON-parses @wirken.event.calls.arguments, extracts `path` and `content`, regexes the extension, and prefix-matches the content head. See datadog/README.md.

@security-team
